findings.title: L1StandardBridge can be reinitialized repeatedly to replace the trusted messenger and seize bridge funds

best_agent_match.title: L1StandardBridge can be reinitialized at any time to swap in an attacker-controlled messenger

findings.title: ERC20 deposits over-credit fee-on-transfer and deflationary tokens, creating insolvent bridge accounting

best_agent_match.title: ERC20 deposits are overcredited for fee-on-transfer / deflationary tokens

findings.title: Unsupported mutable-balance or blocklist-controlled ERC20s can permanently lock bridged withdrawals

best_agent_match.title: Integer Underflow in finalizeBridgeERC20 Allows Permanent DoS of Withdrawals

rejected_candidates.title: Integer Underflow in finalizeBridgeERC20 Allows Permanent DoS of Withdrawals
rejected_candidates.reason: Not reportable as described. In Solidity 0.8 the subtraction reverts atomically, so no underflowed state is stored, and the messenger marks the relay as failed/replayable rather than permanently breaking future withdrawals.

rejected_candidates.title: ETH Can Be Permanently Locked in finalizeBridgeETH Due to Failed Transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: This is expected bridge behavior, not a contract flaw. A failed ETH delivery causes the cross-domain message to fail and remain replayable; permanent lockup only occurs when the chosen recipient always rejects ETH, which is documented and recipient-driven.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation That Deposits Sufficiently Cover Withdrawal in finalizeBridgeERC20
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of the rejected underflow theory. Insufficient deposits cause a normal revert with full state rollback, not a persistent corruption or pair-wide permanent halt by themselves.

rejected_candidates.title: initialize() Function Lacks Access Control Allowing Potential Front-Running
rejected_candidates.reason: Subsumed by F-001. The real issue is stronger: `initialize()` can be called repeatedly at any time because `clearLegacySlot` resets the initializer state.

rejected_candidates.title: Gas Estimation in CrossDomainMessenger May Cause Message Relay Failures
rejected_candidates.reason: Documented UX footgun rather than a protocol vulnerability. Failed relays are an intended replayable state in this messenger design.

rejected_candidates.title: OptimismMintableERC20 Allows Zero Address for Bridge and Remote Token
rejected_candidates.reason: This is deployer misconfiguration, not an exploitable protocol issue in the audited code.

rejected_candidates.title: onlyEOA Modifier Bypass Via Recently Created EOAs
rejected_candidates.reason: Non-reportable. The code explicitly documents that `onlyEOA` is only a best-effort UX guard and is not relied on for security.
